The Lake Charles Refinery uses atmospheric distillation and fluid catalytic cracking (FCC). Atmospheric distillation is the initial step where crude oil is heated and separated into different fractions based on boiling points. FCC breaks down heavier fractions into lighter, more valuable products like gasoline and diesel.
